candidates.stmt:
Clive Grattan has lived in Farnborough for over 30 years and has represented Cherrywood ward on Rushmoor Borough Council since 2012.

Along with fellow Cherrywood councillors Ashley Halstead and Christine Guinness he organises regular surgeries, newsletters, and surveys to keep everyone informed of their work and enable their views to be heard. Clive chairs a non-profit organisation which offers local people help with employment skills; it also supports causes such as local schools and rehabilitation. He is an active participant in a local community garden.

I’m a mum, and I live right here in our community. Two years ago, I decided to stop complaining about what wasn’t working and do something about it by standing as a candidate for Cove and Southwood.

Since then, I’ve been listening to residents, working locally and getting things done, from helping upgrade our playgrounds to bringing people together through community projects.

I chair a local preschool and help lead a community forum that connects local groups and residents, because strong communities don’t just happen, they’re built.

Over the past two years, we’ve started to see real change across Rushmoor. There’s more to do, but we’re moving in the right direction and we can’t risk losing that progress.

Like many of you, I want our area to feel safe, welcoming and something we can all be proud of.

If you feel the same, I’d really appreciate your support.

I have lived in Rushmoor since 2021, and chose to settle here after leaving the Army. I now work delivering real, observable improvements to some of the most complex programmes in the public and private sector across the UK. I want to use this experience to stand up for local residents and businesses.

As your councillor I would prioritise:
-Standing up against austerity and funding cuts imposed by central government
-Tackling the housing crisis and spiralling cost of living - arguing for more investment in public housing and services
-Supporting local businesses to keep public money in our local community
-Ensuring services are delivered for the good of local residents, not to maximise profit
-Safeguarding our environment by tackling flytipping, holding companies to account on environmental damage and protecting our green spaces

Alan Hilliar is bringing something new to political life in Aldershot and Farnborough.

Alan is an experienced business change consultant and has worked with organisations around the country, helping them to use technology more effectively. He and his wife Carolyn live locally and are active in their local church in Ash. He regularly cycles to work in Farnham and enjoys walks with his wife Carolyn and their border collie, Rosie in the local countryside. Outside of work, he’s a talented singer and performs in local amateur musical theatre.

He’s lived and worked in this area for the past fifteen years and has put his energies into bringing about change in our local communities, working with young people, environmental groups and housing issues.

Alan says “The Conservatives have become obsessed with getting the UK out of the European Union at any cost. They have lost the plot and lost touch with the importance of international business, particularly in our local economy.”

“Labour has lost its purpose and direction. They recycle old ideas that no longer relate to the real issues this country is facing. They have no vision for our local community.”

“I want to see us stay within the European Single Market and to maintain the relationships that have helped us grow as an economy.”

“We need a fresh vision to put new life back into our town centres. We need a greater sense of community, pride and identity, celebrating the different traditions which have formed our values in order to build a society which is open, tolerant and free.”

As a life long resident of Farnborough, the future of our community really matters to me. I am standing for election because I am concerned about the future of our town and passionate about giving local people a voice on the council. I have been visiting residents to discuss their views and concerns over the last few months. If I have missed you, you may have seen a copy of my “Here for Rushmoor” newsletter or one of my recent calling cards dropped through your letter box.  I will continue to call on as many people as possible to listen to their views during the run up to the election. However, if I have not managed to speak to you directly yet, or you have an issue which you would like to raise with me, please do feel free to get in touch - my contact details are at the top of this letter. I have also enclosed a copy of my election leaflet which provides more information about my priorities for our community.

This May will be your first opportunity to have your say at the ballot box since the Conservatives crashed our economy. You may have seen the signs yourself in your rent or mortgage and when shopping for food. Our taxes are higher, our NHS and other public services have been paralysed, yet our national debt has increased by over £1.4 trillion. By voting for me, you will send a message to the Conservatives that sticking-plaster policies are not good enough. A Fresh Start for Knellwood is possible, but it starts with you. Please lend me your vote this year and give change a chance. This year in Knellwood is going to be competive more than before. Please lend me your vote this year and give change a chance. Thank you for taking the time to read. I would be honoured to have your support in this election. Yours faithfully, Tad Cragg Labour Party Candidate for St John’s Promoted by Bill O’Donovan on behalf of Tad Cragg, both at 16 Melrose Close, GU14 9US.

I’m honoured to have been selected as a candidate for Manor Park Ward. This feels like a natural continuation of the community work I’ve been committed to in Rushmoor for many years.

As a veteran’s spouse, I understand the realities of military life and the importance of visible, reliable support, particularly in a ward where many military families call home and where a strong sense of community matters.

I currently serve within Manor Park Ward as a Safeguarding Governor at St Michael’s Infant School and as a Safeguarding Officer at St Michael’s Church. These roles have strengthened my understanding of the importance of safe, supportive environments for children, families, and the wider community.

I have also spent many years supporting women and families affected by domestic abuse. Through this work, I have learned to work across services and systems, advocating for those who may feel unheard or overlooked. It continues to shape how I show up, with empathy, care, and a strong commitment to the community.  Supporting others and serving the community has always been something I find deeply meaningful.

I am standing for Manor Park Ward to be a visible, approachable local councillor who, if elected, will listen, take action, and stand up for the residents of Manor Park, with a strong commitment to our community.

Every voice matters.

I’m really proud to be standing as the Labour candidate for Rowhill in the local elections on 7 May 2026.

It’s been a real privilege to support Councillor Lisa Greenway and the work she does for our community, and I’m very grateful to Cllr Alex Crawford for his guidance and support along the way. It’s also been a privilege to meet so many of you over the past year and hear about the issues that matter most to you.

If elected, I’ll continue working closely with Lisa and will always do my best to represent the people of Rowhill. I care deeply about the things that matter locally—keeping on top of potholes with Hampshire County Council, tackling fly-tipping, supporting community safety, and helping keep our neighbourhoods clean and welcoming.

I also understand how important it is that public money is looked after properly, and I’ll work to make sure Rushmoor’s finances continue to be managed carefully and responsibly.

Rowhill really is a special place, with fantastic people, community groups, green spaces, businesses and schools. I’m proud to be part of it, and I look forward to continuing to bring people together and support our community.

Your voice matters, and I’ll always listen.

Hello!
I’m Nick, standing for election as your Green Party candidate for Rowhill Ward of Rushmoor Borough Council.

I’m an Aldershot local boy having been born in the Louise Margaret, and I live in Rowhill Ward, so I’d like to hear what matters to you.
Whilst I’ve lived in Aldershot my whole life, I don’t believe that that is where community stops. If you moved here yesterday, I’d like for Rushmoor to be a wonderful and thriving place to live.

As your councillor I would prioritise:
- Supporting local businesses to keep public money in our local community
- Safeguarding our environment by tackling flytipping, holding companies to account on environmental damage and protecting our green spaces
- Ensuring services are delivered for the good of local residents, not to maximise profit
- Standing up against austerity and funding cuts imposed by central government
- Tackling the housing crisis and spiralling cost of living - arguing for more investment in public housing and services

Thank you for your consideration
Nick Stamp 
Green Party for Rowhill Ward

Dear Resident,

I am your British Democrats candidate standing in Rowhill Ward for the Rushmoor Borough Council elections.

As a Rowhill resident, I care deeply about our community. If elected, I will listen to your concerns and work hard everyday to solve local problems. I'm ready to be a strong voice for my neighbours and deliver the results our ward deserves.

I am a lifelong resident of Farnborough & Aldershot. The people and community are of the utmost importance to me.

​I am the only candidate campaigning against the abolition of Rushmoor Council and our distinct local identity. While other parties support merging Rushmoor into a Unitary Authority with neighbouring councils, I will fight to keep our council independent and as local as possible.

I have 8 positive pledges for Rowhill & Rushmoor:

✅️ End the use of Potters International Hotel for migrant housing

✅️ Put local residents first for housing

✅️ Reduce Council Tax; make efficiency savings

✅️ Tackle Crime & Anti-Social Behaviour

✅️ Protect our green spaces from unnecessary overdevelopment

✅️ Better Support for Veterans & elderly residents

✅ Save Rushmoor Council: Demand a local referendum on the future unitary authority.

✅️ Two hours free parking for residents: Supporting local shops, helping local people

I advocate for positive policies that address housing, the safety of women and children, and the preservation of our local green spaces. I am committed to cutting taxes, tackling crime and anti-social behaviour, and providing robust support for veterans and the elderly, while ensuring locals have a choice regarding the unitary authority. Ultimately, I support local policies that truly work for local people.

Whilst the other candidates represent their own interests and ends; I will serve the interests of the people and needs of the local residents.

​I look forward to meeting as many of you as possible over the coming weeks and hearing your views on how we can improve our community.

​With bes

As a councillor my priorities will be: Investing in our playgrounds and green spaces Campaigning to reverse cuts to the traﬃc management budget which is reducing Rushmoor’s ability to address parking problems. Scrutinising the council’s plans for Farnborough town centre to make sure residents’ money is being spent sensibly. Holding housing associations to account to ensure they respond to tenants promptly and carry out work to a high standard. Keeping you informed and listen to you through regular newsletters, surveys, and surgeries.

I'm Fred and I'm running as a Green Party councillor for St John's.
I grew up in Farnborough so I know the area like the back of my hand. This means I can understand the issues we, especially young folks, face as residents. If elected, I will try my best to tackle them head-on.
As well as this, I recognise the impending multi-crisis owing to climate change, worsening inequality and rising hatred on our streets. I will do what I can to tackle local issues which arise from global headwinds outside of our direct control, and fight upwards to protect our planet and our communities.

1. The only Military Connection to represent you on the council.
2. Works for the local people, not a party. 
3. Good track record after winning the previous two elections. 
Contact details:
nadia.martin38@hotmail.com

I grew up in Blackwater, attending schools and college in Farnborough. I decided to settle in Farnborough and start a family. I took some time out of the workplace to raise my children while my partner went to university to begin her career in the NHS. Most of my working life has been spent in warehousing and distribution. I worked my way to HGV driver before leaving the industry to start my own business. I have always been interested in politics, shouting loudly and often from my sofa. I am positive that there is another way so I decided to get involved. I am passionate about our manifesto and believe it will deliver a sustainable and just society for everyone both now and for future generations. This is why I am standing as your Green candidate in Aldershot on Thursday 4th July.
